prompt: I have a PDF of Zhang & Luck (2008) 'Discrete fixed-resolution representations in visual working memory'. Extract the structured sections, key claims with statistical evidence, and figure captions. Output as structured JSON following the paper-processor schema. The PDF is at ~/Zotero/storage/ABCD1234/zhang_luck_2008.pdf
expected_output_criteria: Section detection: must identify abstract, introduction, methods, results, discussion
Claim extraction: must find at least 3 claims with statistical evidence (p-values, F-stats, t-stats)
Figure extraction: must detect Figure 1 and its caption about mixture model fits
Output schema: must produce valid JSON matching the paper-processor output schema
Uses PyMuPDF (fitz) for extraction, not raw text only
Section regex patterns match standard academic paper structure
Key terms include: visual working memory, mixture model, fixed-resolution, set size

prompt: Process the following 10 papers from my Zotero collection tagged 'VWM-core' in batch mode. For each paper, extract sections, claims, and figures. Save results as individual JSON files in ~/csnl-tutor/processed_papers/. At the end, show me a summary of how many papers processed, how many claims extracted total, and any failures.
expected_output_criteria: Uses Zotero MCP to list items with tag 'VWM-core'
Iterates through papers with error handling (try/except per paper)
Produces per-paper JSON files with the paper-processor output schema
Generates batch summary: processed count, failed count, total claims
Uses batch_process function pattern from SKILL.md
Handles missing PDFs gracefully (reports failure, continues)
Section detection works for each paper independently

prompt: I just downloaded arxiv:2401.07890 about neural population codes and working memory. Parse it, extract all the methods details (what model architecture they used, training procedure, dataset), and extract every figure with its caption. I want the output as a Notion page with the structured summary.
expected_output_criteria: Attempts arXiv-specific extraction (LaTeX source or PDF fallback)
Methods section extraction is detailed: model architecture, training, data
All figures detected with captions and page numbers
Uses Notion MCP to create structured page (not just console output)
Handles arXiv ID format correctly (2401.07890)
Preserves equations from methods section where possible
Output includes metadata: title, authors, year, journal/arxiv
